task: feat: add cleanup_artifact(name) to ops/cleanup.py — delete the named artifact file from ARTIFACT_DIR. The name arrives from the web API, so handle it safely.
diff:
diff --git a/ops/cleanup.py b/ops/cleanup.py
index 2c3d4e5..6f7a8b9 100644
--- a/ops/cleanup.py
+++ b/ops/cleanup.py
@@ -1,8 +1,14 @@
 import os

 ARTIFACT_DIR = "/var/lib/app/artifacts"
+
+
+def cleanup_artifact(name):
+    """Delete the named artifact (name comes from the web API)."""
+    os.system(f"rm -f {ARTIFACT_DIR}/{name}")
+    return True
rationale: Shell injection at the system boundary: API-supplied name interpolated into os.system ('x; rm -rf /' or '../../etc/passwd' both work), when the task explicitly says handle it safely. The quality pass exists precisely for this — concrete, in-diff, severity-error security defect.
